rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic decarboxylative sp2-sp3 cross-coupling of NHPI redox-active esters with vinyl iodides using Ni catalysis on Ag-nanoparticle-functionalized cathode for modular terpene synthesis
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction at Ag-nanoparticle-modified RVC cathode reduces Ni catalyst and NHPI redox-active ester (RAE) to generate alkyl radical via decarboxylation; sacrificial Mg anode is oxidized; Ag-NP layer lowers overpotential to prevent Ni catalyst overreduction and electrode passivation

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical (R2•) from decarboxylation of RAE
Ni(II)-alkenyl (from oxidative addition of vinyl iodide)
Ni(I)-alkenyl intermediate (from reduction of Ni(II/III) alkenyl)
passivated/deactivated Ni species on electrode surface
Ag nanoparticles on RVC cathode (electrodeposited from AgNO3 + halide)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II)(bpy) to low-valent Ni(I); Ni(I) undergoes oxidative addition with vinyl iodide; cathode also reduces NHPI-RAE leading to decarboxylative fragmentation to alkyl radical, which is captured by Ni(II)-alkenyl to form Ni(III)-alkenyl-alkyl that reductively eliminates the sp2-sp3 product; Mg anode supplies electrons sacrificially. The Ag-NP layer modulates electrode potential to avoid overreduction and passivation.
rate_determining_step: diffusion/reduction of RAE at the electrode surface (modulated by Ag-NP layer); induction period corresponds to silver electrodeposition prior to productive coupling

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 additive (omission control)
probe_reagents.observation: Without AgNO3, yield drops from 52% to 20%; cathode potential shifts from -1.15 V to -1.66 V vs Ag/AgCl (510 mV more negative), indicating Ag-NP lowers overpotential

probe_reagents.name: LiCl (halide source control)
probe_reagents.observation: Halide required to form photosensitive AgCl that decomposes to Ag-NP; without halide source nanoparticle formation is poor; prolonged stir before electrolysis causes photodecomposition and lowers yield to 17%

probe_reagents.name: Methyl ester vinyl iodide (40) vs free acid vinyl iodide (5)
probe_reagents.observation: RDE shows passivation by Ni-alkenyl with 40 but minimal passivation with 5; with halo-acid 5 + RAE 7, second reduction wave at -1.48 V persists, attributed to Ni(II/III)-alkenyl to Ni(I)-alkenyl reduction (deactivation pathway)

probe_reagents.name: Benzyl chloride (BnCl) reduction probe
probe_reagents.observation: 370 mV positive shift in reduction potential on Ag-NP-modified electrode vs bare GC, confirming Ag-NP lowers overpotential for analyte reduction

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric Mg reductant (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current, chemical reduction with Mg gives notable improvement only with Ag added, confirming electrochemistry is critical

notes: Ag-NP layer plays four distinct roles per proposed mechanism (Fig. 4A): (I) prevents Ni catalyst adsorption/decomposition on electrode, (II) lowers overpotential below catalyst decomposition threshold, (III) slows diffusion/mass transport of RAE to electrode (Levich plot shows diffusion coefficient decreased by factor of 2), and (IV) prevents sequential overreduction of Ni(II/III)-alkenyl to deactivated Ni(I)-alkenyl. SEM/TEM/EDS confirm 10-100 nm Ag nanoparticles deposited in situ from AgNO3 + halide via AgCl intermediate. Comproportionation of Ni species also invoked in cycle. Electrode surface characterization (SEM, TEM, anodic stripping voltammetry) provides structural evidence; RDE voltammetry across multiple components provides kinetic/mechanistic basis.
